plan_adherence_notes: Diffed against `ff/Tbayes-market-ed8`, the execution follows the planned helper-only scope closely: `backend/server.py` gained a module-scoped process-start timestamp, an `uptime_seconds()` helper, shared `db`/`inference`/`auth` component builders, aggregate status reduction with the planned `ok`/`degraded`/`unhealthy` vocabulary, and a dedicated `v1_health_payload()` that extends `health_payload().copy()` with `version`, `uptime_seconds`, and `components`. It also stayed inside the explicit step boundary by leaving `reset_state()`, `/health`, `/healthz`, `service_index_payload()`, and router wiring unchanged. The only scope addition beyond the title is focused helper-level unit coverage in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`, which is additive and consistent with validating the new contract.
reason: The plan follows directly from the research on field naming, uptime anchoring, reset-state boundaries, live runtime sources, and top-level aggregation semantics; the implementation matches that plan without router or legacy-health scope creep; and the available backend API test suite passed under `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py'` (238 tests, OK).
